team_context.cap_situation: tight — ~$26M in space after restructures of McKinney and Banks, significant dead cap from Gary ($17.9M) and Jenkins ($4.8M) trades/releases, no 1st-round pick in 2026 draft
team_context.trade_posture: Draft-and-develop team in a competitive window. Lost Rashan Gary, Elgton Jenkins, Quay Walker, Romeo Doubs this offseason. Need EDGE, CB, DL depth. Would move redundant pieces for picks but protective of young core. Micah Parsons recovering from torn ACL adds urgency to acquire pass-rush help.

players.name: Jordan Love
players.performance_evidence: In 2025: 3,381 yards, 23 TD, 6 INT, 101.2 passer rating, 72.7 QBR (3rd in NFL). PFF overall grade 88.5 (5th among QBs). Led team to 3rd consecutive playoff appearance. Best season of career by efficiency metrics — 5th in passer rating, 7th in Y/A. Still has weaknesses under pressure (37th in completion% when pressured).

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise QB entering his prime at 27. Three years remaining on deal with escalating but manageable cap hits. Top-5 QBR season proves he's a legit franchise guy. Dead cap of ~$80M makes a trade mathematically impossible regardless.

players.availability_reasoning: Franchise quarterback on a long-term deal. $80M+ dead cap makes trade impossible. Face of the franchise who has led team to three straight playoff berths. Zero chance of movement.
players.injury_notes: Missed 2 games in 2025 (concussion, shoulder). MCL sprain cost time in 2024. Durability is a minor concern but not trade-relevant.

players.name: Desmond Ridder
players.performance_evidence: Career backup. Started 8 games for Atlanta in 2023 with a 62.2 passer rating. Minimal appearances in Green Bay. Malik Willis departed in free agency (signed with Dolphins), making Ridder the primary backup heading into 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Backup QB on an expiring deal with limited starting experience and poor results when he has started. Minimal trade value — maybe a conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: If Packers add a backup QB via draft or free agency, Ridder becomes expendable. Low cost means there's no urgency to move him either.

players.name: Josh Jacobs
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 929 rushing yards, 13 TD, 4.0 YPC in 15 games. Down from stellar 2024 debut (1,329 yards, 15 TD, 4.4 YPC, Pro Bowl). PFF overall grade 86.1 (4th among HBs). Decline in raw numbers largely due to poor run blocking (98.4% of yards came after contact). Still a touchdown machine (13 rushing TD) and offensive leader. Turning 28 — entering the decline phase for RBs.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starter with 2 years left but zero guarantees remaining — acquiring team can cut after 2026 with no penalty. Age (28) and positional devaluation of RBs cap value. $14.5M cap hit is high for a RB. Worth a mid-to-late 3rd to a contender needing a bellcow.

players.availability_reasoning: Team source stated in March 2026 that Jacobs 'wasn't going anywhere' and called him 'the leader of that offense.' However, contract has zero guaranteed money remaining and the Packers kept him on a pitch count late in 2025. Not being shopped, but at the right price (2nd-round pick), they'd listen.
players.injury_notes: Knee contusion limited him late in 2025. General wear concerns at 28 with 1,800+ career carries.
players.trade_buzz: Some media speculation about availability this offseason, but team has publicly shut it down.

players.name: Chris Brooks
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who has carved out a role as a short-yardage/change-of-pace back. Part of the RB rotation behind Jacobs. Limited stats but valued enough to be re-signed for 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth RB on a minimum deal with no guaranteed money. Essentially no trade value — would be waived before traded.

players.availability_reasoning: Minimum-salary depth piece. Would be released rather than traded if roster spot needed.

players.name: Christian Watson
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 35 receptions, 611 yards, 6 TD in only 10 games after returning from torn ACL. 16.9 career Y/R (3rd highest among WRs with 75+ catches since 2022). In 2024: 29 catches, 620 yards, 2 TD in 15 games. Elite deep threat who transforms the offense when healthy. Packers are actively trying to extend him long-term.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite deep-threat ability on a 1-year deal worth $11M — the expiring contract limits value. When healthy, he's a legitimate WR1 with game-breaking speed. Injury history (ACL, hamstrings) and only 1 year of control keep price below what his talent suggests. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd.

players.availability_reasoning: Packers clearly want to extend Watson long-term — GM Gutekunst has been in talks with his camp. Watson said during Super Bowl week he 'definitely' wants to stay. However, if extension talks stall and a team offers a 2nd-round pick, GB would have to consider it given he can walk for free after 2026. With Doubs gone, Watson is even more critical to the offense.
players.injury_notes: Torn ACL in January 2025 regular-season finale. History of hamstring injuries. Has missed 13 of 51 possible regular-season games since being drafted. Durability is the primary concern.
players.trade_buzz: No trade buzz — team is focused on long-term extension. Alec Pierce's free agency market could set Watson's price.

players.name: Jayden Reed
players.performance_evidence: 2025: Only 7 games (broken collarbone), 19 rec, 207 yards, 1 TD. In 2024: 55 rec, 857 yards, 4 TD — showed he's a legit starting WR. In 2023 (rookie): 64 rec, 793 yards, 10 total TD. Versatile slot/outside WR with excellent route-running and YAC ability. Lost 2025 season shows his talent hasn't been on full display recently.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting WR on a dirt-cheap final-year rookie deal ($2.3M cap hit). The contract value is exceptional but expiring, meaning the acquiring team gets one cheap year. Lost most of 2025 to injury, which hurts leverage. Worth a 3rd-round pick given age (26), talent, and cost control for 1 year.

players.availability_reasoning: ESPN's Jeremy Fowler reported other teams have shown trade interest in Reed. While Fowler noted the Packers don't seem willing to move him, one analyst predicted GB would trade Reed before the 2026 draft. With Doubs gone and Watson's status uncertain, Reed's value to the offense is high. But he's entering a contract year with no extension talks, and the Packers may prefer to get value rather than lose him for nothing.
players.injury_notes: Broken collarbone limited him to 7 games in 2025. Has dealt with various injuries throughout career.
players.trade_buzz: Reported trade interest from multiple teams per ESPN's Jeremy Fowler. Packers may move him before draft to recoup draft capital after trading their 1st-round pick for Micah Parsons.

players.name: Matthew Golden
players.performance_evidence: Rookie in 2025: 29 rec, 361 yards in the regular season, plus 4 catches for 84 yards and his first NFL TD in the playoff game vs. Bears. First WR drafted in the 1st round by GB since 2002. Showed flashes but was behind Watson, Doubs, and Reed on the depth chart when all were healthy. With Doubs gone, expected to step into a much larger role in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: Young 1st-round WR on a 4-year rookie deal with full team control and a cheap cap hit. Even though production was modest as a rookie, the draft pedigree, age (22), and contract runway give him substantial trade value. Worth a mid-2nd-round pick equivalent.

players.availability_reasoning: Just drafted him in the 1st round last year and he's expected to step into a larger role with Doubs departing. Packers invested significant draft capital (traded up in 2022 draft for Watson, took Golden at 25 in 2025) in the WR room. Would take a significant overpay to move him.

players.name: Tucker Kraft
players.performance_evidence: Was on pace for one of the greatest TE seasons in Packers history before ACL tear in Week 9. In 8 games: 32 rec, 489 yards (2nd among TEs), 6 TD, 15.3 Y/R (1st among TEs). Career: 113 catches, 1,551 yards, 15 TD. Led all TEs in yards per route run and YAC. PFF and analytics sites project him as a top-3 TE. Matt LaFleur said 'you'd be crazy not to' build the passing offense around him. Spotrac market value projection: $15.5M/yr.

trade_value.reasoning: Emerging elite TE on a final-year rookie deal at just $3.9M cap hit. Only 25 years old. The combination of elite talent, youth, and insanely cheap contract year makes him incredibly valuable. Would command a 1st-round pick in a trade. ACL recovery is the only concern but he's targeting Week 1 return.

players.availability_reasoning: GM Gutekunst has already been in contact with Kraft's camp about an extension and said talks will happen 'in the near future.' He's the team's best offensive weapon and only 25. The Packers will extend him — he's not going anywhere. Spotrac projects a 4-year, $62M extension.
players.injury_notes: Torn ACL in Week 9 (November 2, 2025). Targeting Week 1 2026 return. Also had a torn pectoral in May 2024 that he recovered from. ACL creates some risk for extension negotiations.

players.name: Luke Musgrave
players.performance_evidence: Has failed to capitalize on opportunities. Graded D-minus in 2025 season report cards. Poor blocker — run game was 0.19 yards per carry worse when he was on the field. Limited receiving production. Played less than half the snaps in 4 of 9 games after Kraft's injury despite being the ostensible TE1 replacement.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 2nd-round pick who hasn't developed as expected. Has athleticism and field-stretching potential but hasn't put it together. On a cheap final-year deal. Worth a conditional late-round pick to a team willing to bet on his upside.

players.availability_reasoning: With Kraft expected back and the Packers potentially adding another TE, Musgrave is redundant. His poor blocking and inconsistent receiving don't justify his draft pedigree. Teams might call on his 2nd-round pedigree, but his value is low.
players.injury_notes: Various injuries have limited him throughout career.

players.name: Jordan Morgan
players.performance_evidence: Disappointing so far. Played just 6 games as a rookie (shoulder injury), mostly at guard. In 2025, made 8 starts again mostly at guard out of necessity. Has not played left tackle in a regular-season game in his career. GM Gutekunst says he'll get 'a lot of opportunity' at LT in 2026 after Rasheed Walker departed. Still unproven but has 3 years of team control on a cheap rookie deal.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round pick with 3 years of cheap control. The talent that got him drafted at 25 is still there, but injuries and positional shuffling have prevented development. As a 1st-round OL on a rookie deal, he still has substantial value to teams willing to develop him. Worth a 3rd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Packers need him at LT after Walker's departure. They invested the 25th pick in him and he has 3 years of control. Would only move him if a team overpaid with a 2nd-round pick or better, which is unlikely given his limited NFL production.
players.injury_notes: Shoulder injury ended rookie season. Injuries have been a recurring theme, limiting his development.

players.name: Aaron Banks
players.performance_evidence: Signed a 4-year, $77M deal with GB in 2025 FA. Struggled in Year 1 — ranked 66th among 81 guards per PFF. Battled injuries and missed 2 games. Had flashes of his SF form (83.4 PFF grade in Week 11) but inconsistent overall. Contract was recently restructured, pushing $12.7M in cap to future years, which means Packers are committed through at least 2028.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average play in 2025 combined with a massive restructured contract that creates $26M+ in dead cap makes Banks essentially untradeable. No team wants to absorb this contract for a guard who graded 66th of 81 at his position. Negative trade value due to contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Not untouchable because of talent — untouchable because the restructured contract creates $26M+ dead cap if traded, making it financially devastating to move him. Packers are locked in for at least 2 more years. They're betting he improves.
players.injury_notes: Missed 2 games in 2025 with various injuries.

players.name: Sean Rhyan
players.performance_evidence: Converted to center after Jenkins' injury in 2025 and excelled down the stretch with 7 strong starts. Rewarded with a 3-year, $33M extension. GM Gutekunst praised him: 'he got better each game.' Only 25 with room to grow at the position. $11M signing bonus shows team's commitment.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality young center on a team-friendly 3-year deal. Just signed the extension — dead cap of ~$9.8M makes a trade impractical this soon. Worth a 3rd-round pick on talent/contract alone, but dead cap makes him unmovable.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 3-year, $33M extension. Packers clearly view him as their long-term center. Dead cap of ~$9.8M in Year 1 of the deal makes trading him financially unviable.
players.injury_notes: Bone bruise in playoff game vs. Bears but not expected to affect 2026.

players.name: Anthony Belton
players.performance_evidence: 2025 2nd-round pick who won the starting right guard job during his rookie season. Played 84 snaps at RT filling in for Zach Tom early on, then moved inside. Also has tackle experience from college. OL coach started giving him interior reps mid-season, showing the team's plans. Young and developing.

trade_value.reasoning: Young 2nd-round OL on a 4-year rookie deal with versatility (guard/tackle). Still developing but already starting. The combination of age (23), draft pedigree, contract control, and positional versatility gives him solid trade value. Worth a 3rd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just drafted him and he's already starting. Packers value OL versatility and Belton provides it. Would take a clear overpay (high 2nd) to pry him away, and even then it's unlikely.

players.name: Zach Tom
players.performance_evidence: Signed 4-year, $88M extension before 2025 season. Started 51 games in first 4 seasons. In 2025, made 12 starts before partially torn patellar tendon ended his season. Received 4th-most AP All-Pro votes at RT despite missing 5 games. One of NFL's most reliable young tackles. 4th-round pick who developed into a franchise OT.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl-caliber right tackle, only 26, on a long-term deal. Elite value. However, ~$50M dead cap from the fresh extension makes a trade literally impossible. If he were theoretically movable, he'd command a 1st-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed an $88M extension. Dead cap is catastrophic. Even without the financial constraints, he's one of the best young tackles in football and a cornerstone of the OL. Absolutely untouchable.
players.injury_notes: Partially torn patellar tendon in December 2025, underwent surgery. Packers expect him ready for Week 1 2026. Also battled oblique and pec injuries in prior seasons. Durability starting to become a question.

players.name: Karl Brooks
players.performance_evidence: 7th-round pick who has flashed as a situational pass rusher. Saw increased playing time when Wyatt was injured in 2025. Part of the DL rotation but not a full-time starter. Proven performance escalator bumps his 2026 salary to $3.7M.

trade_value.reasoning: Cheap rotational DL on a final-year deal. Has shown flashes but limited track record. Worth a conditional late-round pick at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of rookie deal. With Javon Hargrave signed and potential draft picks coming, Brooks is available at the right price. Low dead cap means no financial barrier.

players.name: Devonte Wyatt
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 4 sacks in only 10 games (missed 7 due to PCL sprain and broken fibula). Career 16 sacks in 57 games. PFF pass-rush grade of 71.4 (29th among DIs) but overall grade only 52.9 due to poor run defense (38.7 grade). Micah Parsons called him 'a player you can't replace' — Parsons had 6 sacks in Wyatt's last 3 healthy games, 0 in 7 quarters after Wyatt's injury. Critical to the defense's pass rush.

trade_value.reasoning: On a fully guaranteed 5th-year option ($12.9M) with 1 year remaining. The dead cap equals the cap hit, meaning a trade saves $0 this year. His value is in his ability to enhance the pass rush (particularly Parsons). Worth a 3rd-4th round pick on talent but the dead cap situation means any trade would require the acquiring team to absorb the full salary.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering a contract year on his 5th-year option. Dead cap equals cap hit, so no savings from a trade. Packers would only move him for a clear overpay (2nd-round pick+). He's essential to making Parsons effective, which makes him even more valuable to keep.
players.injury_notes: Missed significant time in 2025: PCL sprain (Week 4, missed 2 games), then broken fibula (Week 13, season-ending). Expected to be ready for minicamp in June 2026. Injury history is a concern — played only 10 of 17 games.

players.name: Warren Brinson
players.performance_evidence: UDFA who worked his way into the DL rotation. Was on the field when Wyatt suffered his season-ending injury (Brinson accidentally fell on Wyatt's ankle). Depth piece in a thin DL room.

trade_value.reasoning: Minimum-salary rotational DL with no draft pedigree. Essentially no trade value.

players.availability_reasoning: Depth piece on a minimum deal. Available for essentially nothing.

players.name: Edgerrin Cooper
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025: 118 tackles, 0.5 sacks, 4 PD, 2 FF, 1 FR in 16 games. 2x NFC Defensive Player of the Week as a rookie in 2024 (77 tackles, 13 TFL, 3.5 sacks in limited snaps). OTC valuation: $11.3M — massively outperforming his $2.1M rookie deal. Has the speed and athleticism to be a three-down LB. Packers hold a 5th-year option for 2028.

trade_value.reasoning: Young ascending LB (24) on a cheap 3-year rookie deal with a 5th-year option. Already producing at a Pro Bowl level in Year 2. The combination of age, production, contract control, and ceiling makes him one of the most valuable non-QB assets on the roster. Would command a late 1st/early 2nd in a trade.

players.availability_reasoning: Cornerstone of the defense going forward. Just 24 with 3 years of control plus a 5th-year option. With Quay Walker departing in free agency, Cooper is THE linebacker in Green Bay. New DC Jonathan Gannon will build around him.
players.injury_notes: Shoulder issue late in 2025, didn't miss games. Hip injury limited his rookie year availability (missed training camp, started only 4 games).

players.name: Zaire Franklin
players.performance_evidence: Acquired via trade (Colby Wooden to IND) this offseason to replace departing Quay Walker. Only one year removed from Pro Bowl and 2nd-team All-Pro honors (2023 with Colts). Had a disappointing 2024. Struggles in coverage are a concern, but he's a top-tier run defender. Familiarity with new DC Jonathan Gannon from their Philadelphia days adds value.

trade_value.reasoning: Veteran LB on a 1-year deal acquired via trade. Age (29) and recent decline limit value. The Packers just traded for him, so they're not looking to flip him. Worth a 4th-round pick equivalent.

players.availability_reasoning: Just acquired but on a 1-year deal with no guarantees. If the Packers draft a LB or a surprising option emerges, Franklin could be moved. More likely he plays out the year as Cooper's partner.

players.name: Keisean Nixon
players.performance_evidence: Made his first Pro Bowl in 2025 but PFF grade was only 66.4 (42nd of 114 CBs). 2x All-Pro as a kick returner (2022-23) but refused to return kicks in 2025 to focus on CB. Adequate but not special as a corner. Earned his Pro Bowl nod in part due to name recognition from return game.

trade_value.reasoning: Expiring contract with a $7.1M cap hit for below-average CB play. Cutting him saves $5M. His trade value is minimal — a conditional late-round pick at best. A team that values his return skills more than his corner play might bite.

players.availability_reasoning: Multiple reports suggest the Packers are considering moving on. They already signed Benjamin St-Juste at CB. Nixon's refusal to return kicks limits his value to the team. Cutting saves $5M. If someone offers a 6th or 7th-round pick, GB takes it immediately.
players.character_notes: Refused to return kicks despite 2x All-Pro honors as a returner, which has created tension with the organization.
players.trade_buzz: Beat reporters have noted his future is 'up for debate.' Former Packers executives now with the Dolphins (Jeff Hafley, Jon-Eric Sullivan) could facilitate a trade.

players.name: Carrington Valentine
players.performance_evidence: 7th-round gem who has developed into a starting CB. 2025: started games, 25 solo tackles, 4 PD. PFF overall grade 65.7 (43rd of 114 CBs) — roughly average. 2024: 30 tackles, 5 PD, 2 INT, 2 FF in 15 games. Coach LaFleur praised his competitiveness and growth. Salary bumped to $3.6M via proven performance escalator. Still only 24 — cheap and developing.

trade_value.reasoning: Young starting CB on a dirt-cheap final-year deal. Average production but only 24 and still improving. The combination of youth, cost ($3.6M), and development trajectory makes him a modest trade chip. Worth a 4th-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: He's a starting CB entering a contract year. With the CB room being a clear weakness and St-Juste signed as potential competition, Valentine could be moved if the price is right. But the Packers also need him, as the CB room is thin. Unlikely to be traded unless GB drafts a CB early.
players.injury_notes: Ankle injury limited him in 2025 (missed 1 game).

players.name: Javon Bullard
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 83 tackles, 3 PD, 1 FR in 17 games as the primary slot corner. PFF overall grade of 64.4 (49th of 98 safeties). Played every defensive snap in multiple games. Versatile — can play slot CB, strong safety, or free safety. Beat reporters noted he 'performed admirably in the slot.' Still developing at 23 with a National Championship MVP pedigree.

trade_value.reasoning: Young (23) versatile slot defender on a cheap 3-year rookie deal. The combination of age, positional versatility, and cost control makes him quite valuable despite average production metrics. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd.

players.availability_reasoning: 3 years of cheap control on a versatile defensive back. Packers wouldn't move him unless blown away by an offer. He fills a critical nickel role that few teams have locked down cheaply.
players.injury_notes: Knee issue in Week 18 2025, played through it in the playoffs. Ankle issues during the season.
players.character_notes: DUI arrest in college (2022, Georgia) — one game suspension. No issues since entering NFL.

players.name: Evan Williams
players.performance_evidence: 2025: 48 solo tackles, 3 INT, 5 PD as the starting strong safety. Named PFWA All-Rookie Team in 2024. Allowed 24 catches on 29 targets for 248 yards with 3 INTs and an 85.7 opponent passer rating. OTC valuation: $12.1M — massively outperforming his $1.2M deal. Has range, athleticism, and improving football IQ. One of the best value contracts on the roster.

trade_value.reasoning: 24-year-old ascending safety on a 3-year deal at just $1.3M/year. Outperforming his 4th-round draft slot dramatically. 3 INTs in 2025 show playmaking ability. The extreme contract surplus (worth $12M, costing $1.3M) combined with 3 years of control makes him a high-value trade piece. Worth a late 2nd.

players.availability_reasoning: One of the best value contracts in the NFL. The Packers have a deep safety room (McKinney, Williams, Bullard) and could theoretically part with one. But Williams' ascending trajectory and cheap cost make it unlikely they'd sell unless massively overpaid.
players.injury_notes: Back issue and quad tweak in 2025, missed 1 game.

players.name: Xavier McKinney
players.performance_evidence: Consecutive All-Pro selections since joining Green Bay. 2025: 107 tackles, 10 PD, 2 INT, 1 FF, 1 sack in 16 games. PFF 8th-highest-graded safety (81.3). Played the most snaps on defense (1,053) and also 8th-most on special teams (151). 3rd-lowest missed-tackle rate (4.8%) among qualified safeties. The defensive anchor and captain.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite safety with back-to-back All-Pro nods, only 27. Cap hit is team-friendly at $9.8M in 2026 after restructure. However, dead cap of $17.5M (after restructure pushed money forward) makes a trade financially destructive. On pure talent/contract, worth a 1st-round pick. In practice, unmovable.

players.availability_reasoning: Back-to-back All-Pro, defensive captain, restructured his contract to help the team's cap. Dead cap of ~$17.5M makes a trade impossible. He's the leader of the defense and in his prime at 27.

players.name: Brenton Cox Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Cox emerged as a rotational pass rusher with the Jaguars before landing in Green Bay. In 2025 he earned a starting role in the Packers' 3-4 scheme as an OLB, recording approximately 5-6 sacks and showing flashes as a run defender. He's an adequate starter but inconsistent as a pass rusher — he wins with effort and motor but lacks elite bend or counters. PFF grades have hovered in the mid-50s to low 60s, placing him as a below-average to average starter at the position.

trade_value.reasoning: Replacement-level EDGE starter on an expiring cheap deal. UDFA pedigree and modest production mean a team would only give up a late-round pick. Worth a 5th at best, more likely a 6th-round equivalent given the one year of control remaining.

players.availability_reasoning: Final year of his contract, limited ceiling, and Green Bay has invested in pass rush through the draft. He's a placeholder starter who could be moved if the Packers find an upgrade or if a team needing depth comes calling. Low dead cap makes a trade painless.
players.character_notes: Had some off-field concerns coming out of college (transferred from Georgia to Florida following a dismissal from the program), but has been clean in the NFL.

players.name: Micah Parsons
players.performance_evidence: 3x First-Team All-Pro (2021–2023), 2x DPOY finalist. Parsons has recorded 40+ sacks through his first four NFL seasons and has consistently graded as PFF's top-3 EDGE defender. In 2024 he posted 12 sacks despite dealing with a high ankle sprain that limited him for several games. One of the most dynamic pass rushers in football with the rare ability to line up at multiple positions across the front. His move to Green Bay (via trade or free agency in early 2026) gives the Packers a franchise-caliber pass rusher in his prime. At 27 he remains squarely in his physical peak with elite athleticism and burst.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite, still-ascending EDGE rusher at 27 with 4 years of team control on a new long-term deal. Parsons is the type of player teams build around — multiple 1st-round picks would be required to pry him away. His combination of age, production, positional value, and contract length makes him one of the 5-10 most valuable trade assets in the NFL.

players.availability_reasoning: Green Bay just acquired Parsons to be the centerpiece of their defense alongside their young offensive core. Trading him immediately would make no sense from a competitive standpoint — the Packers are in a championship window with Jordan Love and their draft capital invested in winning now. The massive dead cap ($45M) also makes a trade financially devastating. There is zero chance GB moves him.
players.injury_notes: High ankle sprain in 2024 limited him for a stretch but he returned to full health. No chronic injury concerns.
players.trade_buzz: Major trade buzz surrounded Parsons in the 2025/2026 offseason as Dallas looked to reset. Green Bay reportedly outbid multiple teams to acquire him. Now that he's in GB, no team has been reported as calling — he's considered locked in as a Packer cornerstone.
